highlights.text: I wrote yesterday: “Bear case tomorrow: 6832 has to fail, but in advance of that there is is a complex short on the failure of 6873.” We lost 6832 overnight, broke the range down and went elevator down into this morning down to 6718.
One of those cycles is ES goes elevator down in a vertical flush cutting through every support.
These squeezes often backtest major resistances which were broken during the elevator down sell.
This flush of a big low, trap, and recover refers to my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.

As always, the gap down will result in a Failed Breakdown/squeeze but I would not hold any size over the weekend.” This was highly prescient, and on early Saturday morning, this risk materialized and we gapped down Sunday 45 points.
Like all gap downs/elevator down sells in ES though, this did not take long to see a Failed Breakdown and rip.
The problem, however, is that - despite the big Failed Breakdown - ES remained stuck in the same range for 2 weeks.
Like every elevator down sell though, it would resolve with a short squeeze.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow.

post_title: Another Big Dip In SPX That Was Again Bought. Was That The Bottom? March 4 Plan
